98: **Spain**, top of **Group H** on 7 points and the only side yet to concede a goal at this tournament, face **Belgium** — level with one other team on 5 points from **Group H** — at Los Angeles Stadium in a quarter-final that is only the third competitive meeting between the nations and the second at this stage, with **Belgium** winning the 1986 edition and **Spain** triumphing 2-1 in 1990. Aston Villa midfielder Amadou Onana is ruled out for **Belgium** after rupturing his ACL against the United States, while Leandro Trossard is being assessed for a muscle complaint; Mikel Oyarzabal — who has scored four tournament goals — leads **Spain**'s attack, though Nico Williams (adductor) is again expected to be limited to the bench. **Spain** arrive unbeaten in 35 matches in regulation time, equalling the all-time national-team record, and Mikel Merino's stoppage-time winner against Portugal in the last 16 set up a sixth quarter-final appearance; the winner meets either **France** or **Morocco** in the semi-finals.
97: **France**, top of **Group I** on 9 points after six straight wins — including a 1-0 defeat of Paraguay via a Kylian Mbappé penalty that took him to seven tournament goals, level with Lionel Messi at the top of the Golden Boot standings — face **Morocco**, on 7 points and level with one other team from **Group C**, at Gillette Stadium in a rematch of the 2022 semi-final **France** won 2-0. Aurélien Tchouaméni (adductor) is confirmed out for **France** with Manu Koné keeping his midfield pivot role alongside Adrien Rabiot, while Marcus Thuram — absent against Paraguay with a calf problem — is available from the bench; **Morocco** are without Ismael Saibari (hamstring), who scored in each of their three group games and struck the winning penalty against the Netherlands, forcing Soufiane Rahimi into the starting line-up after his substitute goal sealed the 3-0 win over Canada. Didier Deschamps takes charge of his 25th match at the finals, equalling Helmut Schön's long-standing record, and **France** have won all six all-time meetings with **Morocco** (W4 D2) — a seventh straight unbeaten result would send them into a semi-final against **Spain** or **Belgium** and make them only the third nation to reach three consecutive finals semi-finals; **Morocco** can become the first African nation to achieve back-to-back semi-finals.
